FUN-1: The model represents an island connected to the mainland by a one-way bridge.
FUN-2: The state consists of cars on the island, cars on the bridge in the direction of the island, and cars on the bridge in the direction of the mainland.
FUN-3: The system initializes with no cars on the island, bridge, or mainland.
EQP-1: The environment defines a finite set of cars (CARS).
EQP-2: The environment specifies a maximum bridge capacity, which bounds the number of cars in the system.
SAF-1: The sets of cars are finite.
SAF-2: The total number of cars does not exceed the bridge capacity.
SAF-3: Cars cannot simultaneously occupy both the island and the mainland (one-way constraint).
